teaching.role: Teaching Assistant, Organic Chemistry I & Lab
teaching.description: Mentored 120+ undergraduates; developed supplemental materials improving exam performance by 15%.

summary: PhD chemist/materials scientist with 6+ years in materials characterization, laboratory data quality, root-cause analysis, and DOE-guided process development across polymers, thin films, and functional molecular materials. Experienced translating multi-technique test data into SOP-ready controls, technical recommendations, and reproducible material-quality decisions.

bullets: Lead DOE-guided bio-based polymer process development, defining material-quality readouts, process windows, scale-up specifications, and SOP-ready controls for reproducible laboratory execution.
Translate spectroscopy, thermal-analysis, and process data into technical reports, troubleshooting recommendations, and decision criteria for material selection and process improvement.
Train junior researchers on experimental execution, documentation discipline, and interpretation of characterization data for repeatable results.

Assessed reliability risks in functional electrochromic materials by identifying degradation, delamination, and redox-instability failure modes across 15+ variants using spectroscopy, electrochemistry, thermal analysis, NMR/GPC, and MS.
Built structure-property-processing datasets that connected purity, stability, and performance to experimental conditions, data anomalies, and material-design recommendations.
Documented experimental methods and data-quality checks to support reproducibility, root-cause analysis, and technical communication.
Co-led AFOSR-funded thin-film coating process development, linking CVD/surface preparation to morphology, interface quality, optical performance, and reproducible process controls.
Used first-principles materials reasoning to troubleshoot coating performance and translate process variables into actionable improvements.
Developed and optimized CVD polymer thin-film deposition with DOE strategies and in-situ QCM-D monitoring to quantify growth kinetics and coating reproducibility.

Managed characterization/process-development lab operations for a 15-member group; implemented calibration, SOP, safety, and equipment-tracking systems that improved instrument uptime by 30% and strengthened data quality.

projects.title: Materials Reliability and Failure-Mode Study
projects.description: Mapped degradation, redox instability, and performance failure modes across 15+ functional material variants using orthogonal characterization and stress-test logic.
projects.tech: FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, CV, TGA/DSC, NMR, GPC, MS

projects.title: Bio-Based Polymer Process Qualification
projects.description: Defined DOE-guided process windows, analytical readouts, reproducibility criteria, and SOP-ready material-quality specifications for polymer scale-up.
projects.tech: DOE, SPC, TGA/DSC, spectroscopy, process documentation

projects.title: AFOSR Defense Thin-Film Coatings
projects.description: Established process-property relationships for CVD/surface-preparation workflows and reproducible high-performance coating conditions.
projects.tech: CVD, QCM-D, surface prep, thin films

skills.Materials Testing & Characterization: TGA, DSC, FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, microscopy exposure, NMR, GPC, mass-spectrometry-informed analysis, cyclic voltammetry, QCM-D
skills.Reliability & Failure Analysis: Root-cause analysis, degradation studies, anomalous-data interpretation, coatings/thin-film failure modes, material selection recommendations
skills.Laboratory Quality Systems: SOPs, calibration mindset, reproducibility criteria, test-method documentation, data traceability, technical training
skills.Statistical & Process Methods: DOE, SPC, process windows, technical reports, data visualization, Excel, Python/basic data analysis, JMP/basic
skills.Materials Platforms: Polymers, coatings, thin films, organic/organometallic materials, adhesives/composites adjacency, electrochemical materials
